programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan sets out to identify the mystery man responsible for threatening an unassuming tax lawyer and jeopardising his position at a prestigious legal firm. Guest starring Leah Remini, with Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Amanda's frightened friend does a disappearing act shortly after giving birth to quadruplets, leaving a flustered Jack Stewart holding the babies - and Dr Sloan investigating the activities of an illegal adoption ring. Dick Van Dyke stars

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A woman claims her rock-star husband was killed by an alien - which also impregnated her for good measure. Dick Van Dyke stars

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Team Scorpion attempts to save a man stuck on the edge of a massive sinkhole that threatens to permanently destroy the Los Angeles water supply. Drama, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: A mission to locate sunken treasure in the Pacific goes dangerously awry when Walter and Paige's boat explodes, and they are stranded in the ocean - and surrounded by sharks

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team's mission to track the arsonist targeting US Navy facilities appears to end in success when the man in question is killed by a thermite explosion on board the USS Brewer. Meanwhile, Abby studies evidence relating to fire-starter Bruce Johnson, who died when his car exploded, and discovers coded messages in his quarters that indicate he may have been working as part of a larger gang. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The investigators discover probationary agent Ned Dorneget has been bugged by Harper Dearing, the man behind a spate of arson attacks on US Navy vessels. A phone call from the man himself makes it clear he is out for revenge, and Sam Ryan has information as to the motive behind it. Guest starring Richard Schiff (The West Wing)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team tries to track down wanted terrorist Harper Dearing before another US Navy ship is destroyed, and a desperate Gibbs enlists the aid of a prisoner to draw his target out of hiding. Meanwhile, Palmer is forced to consider putting his nuptials on hold as the stand-off with Dearing becomes heated. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The aftermath of Harper Dearing's bomb attack on the NCIS headquarters dominates proceedings. As rescue crews sift through the debris searching for survivors, Abby, Gibbs, McGee and Vance are accounted for, but Ziva and Tony are trapped in a lift. Dearing makes contact after his ex-wife is taken into custody, enabling his car to be traced to an abandoned lot - and the agents arrive just in time to see the vehicle blow up. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team investigate the death of an armoury employee when divers find her body in a car at the bottom of a lake. The victim had been reported missing since she was evacuated before the blast at NCIS headquarters. Meanwhile, crisis counsellor Miles Wolf carries out psychological assessments of all the agents in the wake of the bombings. Guest starring Steve Valentine (Crossing Jordan)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Team Scorpion attempts to save a man stuck on the edge of a massive sinkhole that threatens to permanently destroy the Los Angeles water supply. Drama, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: CSI: Cyber
programme.desc: A jogger is found dead, and with no-one around to see how she died, the woman's fitness tracker appears to be the only 'witness' to the crime. Making use of their cyber expertise, Avery Ryan and her team crack into the device and retrace the victim's steps for the entire night leading up to her untimely demise. Crime drama, starring Patricia Arquette

programme.title: CSI: Cyber
programme.desc: Notorious cyber-criminal Python returns to kidnap Avery's surrogate daughter, leaving the team to decipher a series of elaborate code-like puzzles in an attempt to save her life

programme.title: CSI: Cyber
programme.desc: The team tracks a vigilante who is taking an extreme reaction to objectionable social media posts by killing off the worst offenders. Patricia Arquette and Ted Danson star

programme.title: CSI: Cyber
programme.desc: The team investigates criminals who hack into a traffic app to direct motorists to remote locations where they can be robbed. Russell is reunited with Greer Latimore

programme.title: CSI: Cyber
programme.desc: The fate of the US government is at stake as Avery and the Cyber team search for the hacker responsible for the largest breach of highly classified data in history

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: Harm contemplates his career prospects at Jag but puts his decision on hold until he has dealt with a child killer who has resurfaced and is about to commit another murder

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: The stars of a soap opera begin receiving threats, and it is not long before Mark and Amanda find themselves caught up in a dangerous dispute. Several cast members of American daytime soap The Young and the Restless guest star as themselves

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A devious daughter sets out to test Dr Sloan's powers of deduction when her father dies. Drama, guest starring Christian J LeBlanc

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan cares for a traumatised girl who holds the key to solving a murder, hoping to save her from the clutches of a felon. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Walter and Sylvester go undercover on foreign soil, entering an international chess competition to extract a US spy from behind enemy lines before her identity is exposed

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: When a simple mission to protect a seed bank goes awry, team Scorpion need to protect Happy, Sylvester and Cabe when they begin to suffer from life-threatening hallucinations

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A prostitute and what seems to be one of her clients are gunned down in a drive-by shooting, and when it emerges that the would-be John was actually a Navy officer, Gibbs and DiNozzo realise the killings are connected Lex Tarlionas, an incomplete operation to bring justice to those responsible for an attack on a US airport in 1979, which left 100 people dead. The main suspect is a former Colombian drugs kingpin known as Tomas Orlando, and the murdered sex worker was about to hand over crucial DNA evidence that could have connected him to the crime, but now that she is dead, Gibbs and the others must find another way to finally bring Orlando in. American drama, starring Mark Harmon and David McCallum

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: DiNozzo teams up with his girlfriend, ATF agent Zoe Keates, to investigate the circumstances surrounding the death of a US Marine in what initially seems like a simple road accident. The team reveals the dead man did indeed die due to a broken neck sustained during a crash, but someone shot at him first. As DiNozzo and Keates pursue their inquiry, they discover the victim's death is connected to a controversial ATF plan to place traceable firearms in the criminal system, and then follow them to the heads of crime organisations. Naval crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US marine visits the home of his former captain, only to find the man has been killed, and the killer is waiting inside the property. While the soldier escapes with his life, the attacker gets away too. It quickly emerges the prime suspect is Qasim, an Afghan translator evidence, and evidence points against him, until it is discovered he has a brother, who is loyal to the Taliban. The subsequent chain of evidence leads Gibbs and Bishop to Afghanistan

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Naval intelligence ensign is killed in an unusual car accident. Gibbs and his team are reunited with old friend Ned Dorneget, who is now working with NCIS cyber operations, and NSA attorney Jake Malloy to investigate the case, and discover a conspiracy involving terrorist recruitment on the internet

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Special Agent Ned Dorneget of the NCIS cyber division joins the team to further their investigation into The Calling, the insidious terror network radicalising youngsters via the internet. When it emerges young suicide bomber Bradley was not, in fact, affiliated with The Calling, Gibbs and his colleagues realise they might be back to square-one. Determined to bring the organisation down, the agents pursue two new leads, an arms dealer who may be supplying The Calling with their signature German S-mines, and another boy given a deadly mission by the terrorists. Guest starring Matt Jones

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Walter and Sylvester go undercover on foreign soil, entering an international chess competition to extract a US spy from behind enemy lines before her identity is exposed

programme.desc: When a simple mission to protect a seed bank goes awry, team Scorpion need to protect Happy, Sylvester and Cabe when they begin to suffer from life-threatening hallucinations

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates when a navy intelligence commander responsible for tracking the movements of a Venezuelan politician disappears. Concerned the abduction may lead to a future assassination attempt, the special agents race to find the missing officer, while protecting the foreign dignitary's life

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Kensi goes undercover as a highly trained cat burglar to stop a sophisticated thief from stealing antiquities from a Naval base. It becomes apparent that the thief is actually trying to steal stealth technology hidden in a vase so he can then sell it on to the Chinese. Daniela Rush stars

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A rocket engine expert is killed, leaving Eric to go undercover to ensure that classified technology remains secure. The rest of the team catches up with the dead man's business partner, as well as his daughter - who suspects murder and even provides a name. Chris O'Donnell and Barrett Foa star

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Deeks is forced to resume an old alias he hates in a bid to protect his best friend and chief informant from arms traffickers who want him dead. Crime drama, starring Eric Christian Olsen, Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team goes in search of a stolen radiotherapy canister that could have been used to make a bomb after a man impersonating a Navy Seal dies after being set on fire. Meanwhile, team leader Hetty Lange has been acting strangely and Callen offers to help with whatever is troubling her. Guest starring Claire Forlani (Camelot), with Chris O'Donnell and Linda Hunt

programme.title: Medium

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: Part one of two. A hot-shot lieutenant under Harm's command opens fire on an armoured car in Yugoslavia - but it turns out the soldiers inside were Russian peacekeepers. Meanwhile, Mac prosecutes a Marine weapons expert. Drama, starring David James Elliott

programme.title: Bondi Rescue

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A terminally ill patient begs for Dr Sloan's help. Ten years before, he was wrongly convicted of his wife's murder - now he wants to find the culprit and prove his innocence before he dies. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Mark takes on a temporary assignment as a physician on a cruise ship, where he finds himself investigating the killing of a passenger with more than his fair share of enemies. Dick Van Dyke stars

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Pursued by the local police, Kimble takes refuge working as a handyman for a widow and her young son, but unintentionally makes an enemy of her suitor

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: The owner of a football team is accused of murdering her husband, an alcoholic gambler who was trying to sell his share of the team for some quick cash

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Walker has to save CD's niece after her ex-boyfriend kidnaps her and embarks on a spree of bank robberies with the girl in tow

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Criminals kidnap a bank employee's baby to force her to provide them with inside information on how to commit a robbery - but two are killed in the ensuing raid and the third escapes without revealing the youngster's location. The team immediately mounts a search, but work is hampered by relentless rain and an unexpected terrorist response drill

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The death of Special Agent Ned Dorneget hits the team hard, and when Gibbs receives a visit from his late colleague's mother - CIA Officer Joanna Teague - he must decide whether to let her help with the investigation into the Calling. Meanwhile, Gibbs hopes his young charge Luke, formerly one of the Calling's radicalised footsoldiers, might be key to bringing the organisation down. However, when the terrorists discover where their defecting comrade has taken refuge, the abduct him and flee to Iraq. The team follows hot on their heels, but it seems Luke's ties to the Calling might be stronger than anyone at NCIS ever anticipated. Naval crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, with guest Mimi Rogers

programme.desc: The team goes in search of a stolen radiotherapy canister that could have been used to make a bomb after a man impersonating a Navy Seal dies after being set on fire. programme.title: Perry Mason

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Lt Col Mann arrives to assist with inquiries into the murder of a marine - a case that becomes personal for Gibbs when he learns one of the witnesses is his ex-wife Stephanie. The team discovers the prime suspect was having an affair with the dead man's spouse, but the case is complicated by the revelation that the victim may have been selling top-secret military information. Starring Mark Harmon

Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.desc: Callen and the team are perplexed by Hetty's sudden resignation and press Vance and their new team leader Lauren Hunter for more information about what happened to Hetty. programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Beth and Karen hunt for a hit-and-run motorcyclist after finding an old man lying injured by the road _ but are they on the right track? Joe reacts badly to Tom's admission about the true nature of his relationship with Beth and the men of Skelthwaite set out to make the perfect curry, but find they can't manage without the culinary expertise of eight-year-old Molly. Drama, starring Kerrie Taylor and Julian Lewis-Jones

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Anna tries to help new factory worker Linda Sargent, a single mother afraid she'll lose custody of her five children if it becomes known that she has Parkinson's disease. Meanwhile, Beth agonises over whether to accept the offer of a job in Holland, uncertain of how such a drastic move would affect her relationship with Tom - and paradoxically, it takes a rugby match to help her decide. Lesley Dunlop, Leslie Ash and Philip Middlemiss star

programme.title: Where the Heart Is

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Drama about community nurses living and working in a small Yorkshire town

Period adventure, starring Sean Bean, Daragh O'Malley, Abigail Cruttenden and Cecile Paoli

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: Oz is forced to take radical action to ensure the boys do not come out of the bridge job penniless, but it seems unlikely that his unique brand of sexual charm will save the day - and his doubts about son Rod's masculinity do not do much to help his focus. Just as they seem doomed to fail, a mysterious stranger arrives. Dick Clement and Ian La Frenais's comedy drama, with Tim Healy, Jimmy Nail and Timothy Spall

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: Joe Saugus throws the lads a monetary lifeline, offering to buy the transporter bridge on behalf of his people, while Barry has a final showdown with Kadi and Tatiana. Believing their financial worries are finally over, the lads set off to oversee the construction process in Arizona - where a hostile reception awaits them. Timothy Spall, Jimmy Nail, Tim Healy and Bill Nighy star

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracy and Sharon discover they have been mentioned in an Irish relative's will, but when they arrive on the Emerald Isle they are disappointed to find they have inherited a field. Final episode of the comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Boycie faces his worst nightmare when Marlene's sister decides to pay a visit from Peckham. As if the man-eating glamourpuss was not enough to cope with, he finds all his staff tied up in the barn - and realises his past has come back to haunt him. Guest starring Paula Wilcox

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: A visit to a local country mansion reveals Boycie's admiration for the long-established tradition of respect for the gentry. Elgin moves into Bryan's caravan after being thrown out by his wife, leading to relentless gossiping by Mrs Cakeworthy. When the mobile home burns down Marlene offers the two men a place to stay, much to her husband's chagrin. John Challis and Sue Holderness star

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Boycie and Tyler are united in their distaste when Marlene demands that the family pays a visit to her mother in Bournemouth. programme.desc: Part one of two. programme.title: The Bill

programme.desc: A faith healer divides the Thrift into believers and non-believers, and Dr Marsham is particularly upset by his interference

programme.desc: Robert serves on the Re-Vaccination Committee. When Emmaline comes to the Bramwells' house for help, they suspect she may suffer a reaction to Finn's vaccination experiments

programme.desc: There is joy in the air as Robert Bramwell and Alice prepare to wed. Unfortunately for Eleanor, his impending move to Alice's sumptuous Kew residence also threatens her livelihood

Unfortunately, as the stakes get higher, Connor finds himself gambling more than money - with dramatic consequences for himself and those around him. Feature-length drama, with Bernard Hill and Sylvestra Le Touzel

programme.desc: Sister Ursula insists the midwives do not become emotionally involved with their patients, but her harsh new approach proves hard to cope with in the face of two devastating cases. A father-to-be is badly injured in an explosion, leaving him unable to work to support his family, and a couple with dwarfism expecting their first child are warned that their child may be stillborn, a situation that leaves Patsy confronted with one of the hardest decisions of her life

programme.title: The Last Post
programme.desc: Drama about military police and their families faced with political and personal conflicts in a 1960s British colony on the Red Sea coast. An officer and his wife arrive in Aden, where he is to replace a much-loved captain. He soon has to adjust to life on the frontline, along with resentment among the men that he was appointed instead of the already-present Lt Ed Laithwaite. Honor is taken under the wing of Laithwaite's wife, Alison, who had been having an affair with Captain Page. Starring Jessie Buckley and Jeremy Neumark Jones

programme.title: The Last Post
programme.desc: Mary goes into labour while her husband has left the city to rendezvous with the SAS, who are returning to Aden after a secret mission to capture a dangerous fugitive. programme.desc: William's secretary calls in sick, so Hester volunteers to run the office in her place. However, when an important new client shows up for an appointment she finds herself having to fill in for her missing husband. Comedy, starring Anton Rogers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Harvey decides that if Diana and Tom are seen to be mentally incompetent it will give him power of attorney over their affairs and end all his woes. However, the couple are wise to him, and plan a well-orchestrated display for the visiting psychiatrist. Comedy, with Graham Crowden, Stephanie Cole and Daniel Hill

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy, Clegg and an enthusiastic Compo plot to save Smiler from the terrible fate of being Nora Batty's ex-lodger

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: The final episode of the detective drama sees Gently testifying against corrupt Met officers as he prepares to retire from the force. However, he is persuaded to take on one final investigation - re-opening the case of a woman who was murdered four years previously. Gently's search for the truth leads him to investigate a reforming politician who is tipped to become the next prime minister and uncover a web of political intrigue that places Bacchus and Rachel in danger. Starring Martin Shaw, Lee Ingleby and Lisa McGrillis

programme.desc: Ted leads an investigation into the apparent suicide of a city trader who was embroiled in the cut-throat world of London's financial district in the 1980s. As the team delve deeper into the macho fraternity of 'The Wolfpack', they find a darker side to the debauchery, secret hand signals and all-nighters that bonded the traders together - including a strict hierarchy that might be worth killing for. Meanwhile, Steve tries to learn some tricks of the trade, amid his own financial crisis. Detective drama, starring Larry Lamb

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Double bill. A rising star in the Essex police force is found beaten and shot in his burnt-out car in what proves to be an emotional case for his colleagues. The team helps DCI Jim Sullivan, the detective charged with bringing the killer to justice, and it isn't long before suspicion falls on Dean Fallon - a local drug dealer and the son of a known crime boss. But when he proves to have a solid alibi, the investigation shifts closer to home. Starring Emilia Fox and David Caves, with James Alexandrou and Jason Watkins

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: St Helens v Leeds Rhinos (Kick-off 2.30pm). Mark Chapman presents all the action from the third-round tie at Totally Wicked Stadium. The Rhinos won this competition last season, edging out Salford Red Devils 17-16 in the final, but they face the toughest of tests this afternoon against the reigning Super League champions, who perhaps surprisingly, have not lifted this trophy since 2008

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special edition of the quiz show, in which Bonnie Langford, Arthur Smith, Annabel Giles, Tony Hawks, Diane Louise Jordan, Melinda Messenger, Les Dennis and Richard Herring try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a music-based quiz show in which a father and daughter from Middlesbrough, two forensic scientists from Oxfordshire, and best friends from London must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. Across three rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits from across the years. Whether it's a song from the swinging 60s or the number-one track in today's charts, the teams must prove their ability to recognise songs from all genres of music - from pop to country, rock to R&B and many more

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Celebrity Investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond, Amanda Holden and Danny Jones assist two contestants from Sheffield in picking out the best singers and winning up to £10,000

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jan reaches her breaking point when Ffion is attacked by thugs working for Ross - who warns her that the next attack will be worse if she does not deliver the drugs as ordered. During a visit to the prison to drop off the drugs, she is drawn into helping a prisoner who has been stabbed. While witnessing a broken marriage, Dylan realises it is time to move on, and David realises that Rosa is not coping with Ollie's condition

programme.title: Keeping Faith
programme.desc: Focusing on Osian's case and keeping Rose away from her family consumes Faith's attention, and she loses sight of the effect the divorce is having on her own children. Alys joins a demonstration against the working practices at a local farm, and ends up in trouble with the police. Rose hatches a plan to frame Evan for Gael Reardon's murder, and Faith finally gives in to her feelings for Steve

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches - Manchester City v Leeds United at Etihad Stadium, Liverpool v Aston Villa at Anfield and Crystal Palace v Chelsea at Selhurst Park. The Citizens were looking to further strengthen their position at the top of the table, while the Reds were looking for revenge against the Villans after a stunning 7-2 defeat in the reverse fixture, and Chelsea were aiming to maintain their good form under Thomas Tuchel

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker and is joined by Micah Richards and Alan Shearer to count down the top 10 Premier League transfer bargains

programme.title: Z for Zachariah (2015)
programme.desc: A woman lives alone on a farm that belonged to her family in the aftermath of a nuclear apocalypse, believing herself to be the last survivor of the human race. Her simple life is disrupted by the appearance of a second survivor, and they adapt to working together - but the arrival of a third individual threatens everything they have built. Sci-fi drama based on Robert C O'Brien's novel, starring Margot Robbie, Chiwetel Ejiofor and Chris Pine

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches - Manchester City v Leeds United at Etihad Stadium, Liverpool v Aston Villa at Anfield and Crystal Palace v Chelsea at Selhurst Park. The Citizens were looking to further strengthen their position at the top of the table, while the Reds were looking for revenge against the Villans after a stunning 7-2 defeat in the reverse fixture, and Chelsea were aiming to maintain their good form under Thomas Tuchel

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region

programme.title: Sunday Morning Stories
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley shares her favourite tales of faith, hope and spirituality, when ordinary people do extraordinary things

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: An update on an unusual project in Capel Le Ferne in Kent that Lucy Alexander first visited in 2009. Plus, a mid-terrace house in Liverpool and a cottage in Plaistow, east London

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents a double edition of the antiques hunting show from the East of England Showground in Peterborough, and finds time to visit Blakesley Hall in Birmingham

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: This special Songs of Praise pays tribute to and remembers His Royal Highness The Prince Philip, Duke of Edinburgh. Aled Jones looks back at the Duke's long life, the role faith played in it and his sense of duty, dedication and love for Queen and country. With interviews from leading faith figures and those who knew him well, including the former Dean of Westminster and Gyles Brandreth. We explore Prince Philip's legacy including meeting some of the many millions of young people who have taken part in the Award Scheme bearing his name

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman is on a property search in rural Somerset, helping a couple invest their £500,000 in a home that strikes the balance between country charm and practical open planning

programme.title: Queen of Katwe (2016)
programme.desc: The life of a poor Ugandan youngster is changed for ever when she is introduced to the game of chess. Drama, starring David Oyelowo, Lupita Nyong'o and Madina Nalwanga

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the wildlife of Europe, a crowded continent hiding the most surprising animals in pockets of wilderness. Gibraltar Nature Reserve is home to Europe's only wild population of primates, with its Barbary macaques living a life of kidnapping and high drama, while in the cemeteries of Vienna graverobbing European hamsters do battle with one another. Italian mountain villages are the hunting grounds for rarely seen wolves, and lynx lurk in the forests of Spain

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Tom Heap and Margherita Taylor look at the impact of canals on the history of the British countryside. They the Grand Union Canal and celebrating the 200th anniversary of the Regent's Canal, a once-commercial route that now brings a slice of countryside to the city, benefitting both residents and wildlife. Plus, wildlife cameraman Jack Perks documents some frogs, and an update on the fortunes of a family staking their future on the success of high-tech farming

programme.title: British Academy Film Awards
programme.desc: Dermot O'Leary and Edith Bowman host the annual movie awards live from the Royal Albert Hall. Nominees include Sound of Metal, Promising Young Woman, Rocks, Soul, The Trial of the Chicago 7, and Mank, while performers in the running include Anthony Hopkins, Vanessa Kirby, Riz Ahmed, Frances McDormand, Chadwick Boseman, Clarke Peters, Alfre Woodard, Daniel Kaluuya and David Attenborough.

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: DCI Jo Davidson faces increasing pressure from all sides as AC-12 makes major breakthroughs in the investigation. Hastings acknowledges that the disciplinary action against him put Kate and Steve in a difficult position, and a forensic report from Farida's house results in a shock

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester United at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. Spurs thrashed United 6-1 in October's reverse fixture, but it is the Red Devils who have enjoyed the stronger league campaign overall so far this term. Plus, Burnley v Newcastle United, West Ham United v Leicester City and Sheffield United v Arsenal

programme.title: Once Were Lions
programme.desc: Documentary following the rugby league Great Britain Lions' tour of New Zealand and Papua New Guinea, as they fight for their sporting future

programme.title: This Is My House
programme.desc: Bill Bailey, Emily Atack, Judi Love and Jamali Maddix are joined by guest panellist Jimmy Carr to identify the real Gemma from a line-up of four, all claiming to own the same house in Farnham, Surrey. The house may be easy to spot with its colourful cladding and bright interior, but the real Gemma is not. One says she has gone to town with the decor after becoming newly single, another claims she decorates her house to be 'liked' on social media, the third is a die-hard rainbow fan and the fourth is out to impress to her neighbours. Hosted by Stacey Dooley

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty returns with the programme exposing cases of fraud. Insurers combine forces to catch a serial fraudster making fake claims off the back of high-profile tragedies, while digital data reveals that a claim for £35,000 worth of stolen jewellery is a complete lie

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Documentary following Scottish Ambulance Service crews in Glasgow and Edinburgh as they respond to 999 calls. In a remote rural location, a small child falls in a loch, triggering a huge emergency response, while a motorway crash sees several people hospitalised, including a pregnant woman

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The Rich brothers are joined by Arit Anderson to create an all-year-round garden in Byfleet, Surrey, for stylish octogenarian Gay to enjoy the birds and remember her late husband

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A couple from Essex need Nicki Chapman's help to find a new home in Somerset. Nicki also visits a local farm to sample some homemade specialities made from an ancient grain

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts the culinary contest. Chefs Anna Haugh and Ellis Barrie helping a brother and sister make a meal in 20 minutes that uses familiar ingredients in a new way

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Carpenter Will Kirk puts his carving skills to the test as he tackles a Scandinavian folk figurine that has been mauled by a mischievous puppy. Julie and Amanda work on a 1960s stuffed monkey who has seen better days, and Dominic faces the heavy duty task of unseizing a rusty old metal vice

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: A party election broadcast by the UKIP candidate for the London mayoral election .

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: In-depth current affairs report covering a story behind the headlines

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As Kush plans a huge surprise for Whitney, his mood is brought down when Gray reminds him that by accepting Phil's extra charges he will be going to prison for a very long time. Keegan proudly sets up his new market stall - but what is Tiffany hiding? Karen receives an unusual proposition but takes it the wrong way

programme.title: Fawlty Towers
programme.desc: Basil decides to enhance the hotel's reputation with an exclusive night of gourmet cuisine. His new chef has a cordon bleu rating and loves every type of dish - but he is particularly taken with Manuel. Classic comedy, written by and starring John Cleese and Connie Booth. First broadcast in 1975

programme.title: Greta Thunberg: A Year to Change the World
programme.desc: Documentary following the climate activist over the course of 2019 and 2020, as she travels the world exploring the science of global warming and challenging world leaders to take action on the growing crisis. The programme follows the impact of Covid-19 on her campaign, and the challenges of persuading a world living through one crisis to face up to the possibility of another on the horizon. In the first programme, Greta travels through North America on her way to a UN climate conference in Chile

programme.title: Meet the Khans: Big in Bolton
programme.desc: The couple clash at home before a big family trip to New York. Back in Bolton it's 'new hair who dis' for Faryal before an emotional chat about Amir's plans for one last dance

programme.title: Meet the Khans: Big in Bolton
programme.desc: Amir puts a pause on training for a quick trip to Dubai for some holiday home shopping. Back in Bolton, the wedding hall is causing more headache and Amir fears the worst.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Tom Cruise, Jennifer Connelly and Miles Teller join Graham to talk about the much-anticipated release of Top Gun: Maverick. Felicity Jones discusses her romantic drama The Last Letter from Your Lover, Wunmi Mosaku chats about Bafta-nominated His House, and John Bishop promotes his tour Right Here, Right Now. Plus, music from Years & Years - fronted by It's a Sin's Olly Alexander

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Swimming great Rebecca Adlington, former England cricketer Jonathan Trott, Great Britain sprinter Harry Aikines-Aryeetey and Olympic hockey gold medallist Kate Richardson-Walsh join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: The team builds a Harry Potter-themed den for Kiran, a nine-year-old who has recently had to change schools and move house to help look after his mother, who has mobility problems

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: A fan-club takeover of the programme

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall heads to the rainforest in Ecuador in search of some of the deadliest creatures

programme.title: Being
programme.desc: Roman Catholics Maria and Peter have their fifth child baptised in a traditional Latin service and in Stockton-on-Tees family and friends pay their last respects to June, one of the first women to be ordained as a priest by the Church of the England in 1994

programme.title: Golf: The Masters
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents action from the second day of the Major, taking place at Augusta National in Augusta, Georgia, as the players involved looked to make the cut. With commentary from Andrew Cotter, Ken Brown, Iona Stephen, Mark James and Iain Carter

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents the cookery show featuring guest chefs and celebrities as well as highlights from the BBC Archives and wine choices for the dishes prepared in the studio

programme.title: Recipes That Made Me
programme.desc: Nisha Katona travels to Greater Manchester to discover family recipes from Bangladesh. First, Nisha meets restaurant owner Shahin, who is trying to introduce more traditional Bangladeshi dishes to his customers. Later, Nisha meets Liza, who teaches her how to make an authentic Bangladeshi fish curry

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's football, including Liverpool v Aston Villa and Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester United

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three chefs are halfway through their heat and the pressure is mounting. Once again, they each serve a unique take on the brief celebrating British invention and innovation. Whose inventions in the kitchen will veteran judge Paul Ainsworth consider the best? Only two will proceed to Friday's judging and have a chance to represent the London and the South East region at the regional finals. Andi Oliver presents

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Highlights of the best finds by experts Anita Manning, Mark Stacey and others during the programme's travels around the country. Items featured include a set of Beatles autographs and a collection of Matchbox toys. Plus, presenter Paul Martin visits the birthplace of novelist and playwright DH Lawrence

programme.title: Live Formula E
programme.desc: The Rome ePrix. Live coverage of the second meeting of the campaign from Circuito Cittadino dell-Eur in Rome. Mitch Evans beat Andre Lotterer into second place here last season to claim both his and Jaguar Racing's maiden victory

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: Catalans Dragons v Wakefield Trinity (Kick-off 5.00pm). Mark Chapman presents coverage of the third-round tie at Totally Wicked Stadium in St Helens. Catalans have won the last six meetings between the sides including a 36-24 win in last season's competition, and lifted this trophy for the first and only time in 2018, while Wakefield have not won the competition since 1963. With commentary by Matt Newsum

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: An intimate portrait of a family-run roadside recovery business in the Highlands, as they rescue stranded motorists on some of the most remote roads in Britain

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: The platoon defends headquarters in an exercise against members of the Highland Regiment, leaving Captain Mainwaring and his men with little choice but to spy on their rivals to try to come up with a plan to defeat them. Comedy first broadcast in 1969, starring Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier

programme.title: BAFTA Film Awards Opening Night
programme.desc: Clara Amfo and Rhianna Dhillon host from the Royal Albert Hall as the British Academy celebrates the hair and make-up artists, casting directors, costume and production designers who have been instrumental in creating the best films of 2020

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1987
programme.desc: Some of 1987's biggest music stars look back on the year, which saw Margaret Thatcher secure a third term as prime minister, while the Channel Tunnel began construction and Britain was battered by the Great Storm. While those events unfolded in the outside world, the TOTP studio provided a haven for those who wanted to escape. Stock, Aitken and Waterman provided the floor-fillers as hip-hop and rap made their mark on the chart alongside the homegrown House genre. Contributors include Terence Trent D'Arby, Curiosity Killed the Cat, Rick Astley, Kim Appleby, Andy Bell, Carol Decker and Belinda Carlisle

programme.title: A Life in Ten Pictures
programme.desc: Tupac Shakur's image is known around the world as he was one of hip-hop's most photographed figures. But could just a handful of photos uncover new truths about someone we think we know? This edition throws a lens onto an extraordinary life by focusing on 10 defining pictures, from famous shots to private snaps, with their secrets revealed by those who were there, and those who knew the hip-hop star best

programme.title: Hip Hop at the BBC
programme.desc: Hip-hop performances through the decades from the BBC archives, including the Sugarhill Gang in 1979 and Run DMC, LL Cool J and Eric B & Rakim in the '80s. Plus, Ice T, Monie Love, Fugees and the Roots in the '90s and latterly Dr Dre featuring Eminem, Dizzee Rascal and Jay-Z

programme.title: Golf: The Masters
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents action from the third day of the Major, taking place at Augusta National in Augusta, Georgia. With commentary from Andrew Cotter, Ken Brown, Iona Stephen, Mark James and Iain Carter

programme.title: Finding Jack Charlton
programme.desc: Gabriel Clarke presents a profile of the World Cup-winning footballer, looking at his special relationship with Ireland after becoming the national team's manager, his complicated relationship with brother Bobby, and his final battle with dementia. including contributions from his wife Pat and son John, alongside celebrated Irish players, musicians, writers and politicians, including Larry Mullen, Roddy Doyle, Brendan O'Carroll, Paul McGrath, Niall Quinn, Mick McCarthy, David O'Leary and Andy Townsend

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don adds self-sown ornamental grass seedlings to the paradise garden at Longmeadow, and Advolly Richmond explores the extraordinary history of lawns. Plus, Adam Frost explores the front gardens in a London street

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison presents the show from the Golden Triangle in Gloucestershire, an area famous for wild daffodils. She talks to local people who remember picking the wild flowers as children and visits some of the finest medieval wall paintings in England. Charlotte Smith investigates the challenges facing rural churches and the communities they serve, and a mother and son reveal how they have fulfilled their dreams of farming in the Cumbrian hills

programme.title: The Edible Garden
programme.desc: Gardeners' World presenter Alys Fowler demonstrates how to grow salad ingredients in the garden, including tomatoes, lettuce and cucumbers, but a blight hampers proceedings. She then prepares lime-leaf salad, dandelion fritters and nettle beer

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Carole Baxter and Mairi Rattray create a bargain border from scratch, Kirsty Wilson creates a recycled birdhouse with its own living roof, and Brian Cunningham and his family make family-friendly, no-dig vegetable plots in the lawn

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest scoring chefs from the London and the South East region cook their six-course menus again. They serve food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton, broadcaster Rachel Khoo, and guest judge, Professor Chris Jackson, a geologist and adventurer. Andi Oliver hosts

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights from throughout the series, offering viewers a chance to revisit some of their favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Golf: The Masters
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents action from the third day of the Major, taking place at Augusta National in Augusta, Georgia. With commentary from Andrew Cotter, Ken Brown, Iona Stephen, Mark James and Iain Carter

programme.title: Live Rowing
programme.desc: Matthew Pinsent presents live coverage of the regatta from Varese, Italy. The event will see Great Britain competing for the first time since the 2019 World Championships, and double Helen Glover is expected to be among their ranks for the first time since claiming her second Olympic gold medal at the 2016 Rio Games. With analysis from Katherine Grainger, and commentary by Garry Herbert and James Cracknell

programme.title: Escape to Athena (1979)
programme.desc: Second World War Allied PoWs incarcerated on a Greek island plan to defy the SS by breaking out of prison and pulling off an art robbery, with the help of their liberal-minded camp commandant. Action adventure, with Roger Moore, David Niven, Telly Savalas, Elliott Gould, Stefanie Powers and Claudia Cardinale

programme.title: Remarkable Places to Eat
programme.desc: Another host of leading food experts take Fred Sirieix to their favourite restaurants to explore what makes them so special. In this edition, Chris Bavin takes Fred on a culinary trip around the New Forest, where fresh local produce is king, featuring wood-fired cooking, pork pies and a country pub serving a special Ploughman's

programme.title: Down the Mighty River with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: The naturalist travels down the Baliem River in New Guinea from its source to the sea, hoping to encounter the ancient tribes and dangerous wildlife that live long its banks and in the surrounding jungle. He begins his journey inland at Lake Habbema, and braves some of the world's fiercest white water rapids. Along the way, he stays in a village belonging to the Dani tribe and ventures into some previously unexplored caves

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Kirsty and Aimee gather the flock in from St Ninian's Isle, and inspectors arrive to check Raymond and Johnny's subsidy claim. Meanwhile, Lynn and Sandra host their first workshop at the croft, and 'the Beast from the East' wreaks havoc on Stevie's buffalo farm

programme.title: Climate Change: Ade on the Frontline
programme.desc: Ade Adepitan scours the globe for solutions to environmental problems, examining the natural and technological fixes that can help people slow the rate of decline and adapt to shifts already taking place. He begins in the Solomon Islands and then travels down the east coast of Australia from the Great Barrier Reef to Tasmania, learning about the 'feminisation' of green turtles and coming face-to-face with the terror of a bushfire

programme.title: Australia: Earth's Magical Kingdom
programme.desc: Travelling from the peaks of the Snowy Mountains to tropical Queensland, cameras reveal how animals have learned to thrive across the continent's harsh and beautiful extremes. The nation's unique wildlife includes a tree-dwelling kangaroo, a spider that survives underwater and a bird that gathers burning sticks and embers from bush fires to drop into dry grass to spread a curtain of flame that flushes out insects and small prey. Narrated by Barry Humphries

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Shooting Joe Exotic
programme.desc: Louis revisits his 2011 documentary America's Most Dangerous Pets, returning to Oklahoma to explore what has happened since. Joe Exotic, aka Joseph Allen Maldonado-Passage, now resides in a federal prison after being found guilty of a murder-for-hire plot and multiple animal cruelty charges, and having been the subject of hit Netflix documentary Tiger King

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1987
programme.desc: A celebration of the biggest songs from 1987. Performers include Rick Astley, Whitney Houston, T'Pau, Mel & Kim, Curiosity Killed the Cat, Eric B & Rakim, Pet Shop Boys, Alison Moyet and Boy George

programme.title: Golf: The Masters
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents action from the fourth and final day of the Major, taking place at Augusta National in Augusta, Georgia. With commentary from Andrew Cotter, Ken Brown, Iona Stephen, Mark James and Iain Carter

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Noah invites Claire over for Thanksgiving, but the festivities are spoiled by the arrival of his ex-wife Sandra, who turns up with a new boyfriend in tow. Elsewhere, the Petrellis are caught off guard by the arrival of a surprise dinner guest, and Samuel holds Hiro captive as Lydia tries to discover one of the carnival's secrets

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Peter confronts Sylar and discovers that extreme measures are necessary to stop him. Noah receives unexpected visitors and Claire's voyage of discovery takes her to an unexpected destination. Meanwhile, Samuel's sinister plan enters its final phase

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: The truth is out about Reyhan's abuse, although Sahira is adamant that her father is innocent, leaving Hanssen feeling compromised. Louis discovers old friend Vicky is caught in a toxic relationship and vows to help. But who can he turn to? Kian tries to convince Lucky that his drug taking is a thing of the past

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: Rav Wilding reveals how a team of 'super recognisers' - police officers with an extraordinary ability to recognise faces - could be the key to catching criminals who commit multiple offences

programme.title: Inside the Zoo
programme.desc: The sex of a penguin can only be determined by a blood test, so the penguin keepers' annual sweepstake is launched to guess the sex of all the new youngsters. However, they've noticed that young Nessie has a spinal deformity and the vets are called in to investigate. New babies at both Edinburgh Zoo and the Highland Wildlife Park are keeping everyone busy - not just penguins, but lion cubs, caribou deer and parrots

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: The Masters 2019: Tiger Roars Again
programme.desc: A look back at Tiger Woods' memorable victory at Augusta National Golf Club, which saw the American win this tournament for the fifth time in his career, and raise his overall tally of Major victories to 15. Woods is second only to Jack Nicklaus, who triumphed six times at this famous golf course, and finished with 18 Major titles to his name

programme.title: Golf: The Masters
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents action from the fourth and final day of the Major, taking place at Augusta National in Augusta, Georgia. With commentary from Andrew Cotter, Ken Brown, Iona Stephen, Mark James and Iain Carter

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary capturing the beauty of butterflies close up, and revealing how they inspire the images of street artist Nick Walker and the performances of burlesque dancer Vicky Butterfly. The programme also raises the question of whether a passion for these elegant insects could help people preserve much-loved landscapes. Narrated by Imelda Staunton

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and the team value antiques in Southwell Minster, near Nottingham. Experts Michael Baggott and Mark Stacey try to find the best collectibles inspired by nature, while Paul learns how the green man sculptures that cover the minster chapter house first took their inspiration from ancient pagan beliefs - which could have links with local legend Robin Hood

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Maggie Aderin-Pocock, the Rev Richard Coles, Stuart Maconie and Lou Sanders go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Saved and Remade
programme.desc: Among the radical transformations are disused sideboard that is turned into a special item for a family, and an ornate clock of huge sentimental value. Sabrina Grant presents

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison create virtual reality redesigns for a spacious Wiltshire home that used to be a hair salon - so is poorly designed for actually living in, with a ground floor that feels like two separate properties, each with its own front door. Laura gives the building a welcoming entrance while making the interior family-friendly and functional, while Robert comes up with an innovative approach that cuts down on the amount of floor space but which could radically alter the way the owners live

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys hosts the third semi-final of the quiz, with four more returning contenders answering questions on specialist subjects of the films of Powell and Pressburger, the Bauhaus movement, Emil Zatopek, and the Liberal Democrats, before they each are teted on their general knowledge

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys hosts the fourth semi-final of the quiz, with four more returning contenders answering questions on the specialist subjects of REM, Father Ted, David Niven, and John Wesley, before they are each tested on general knowledge

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti visit a hotel in South Africa that has been built into a 187-foot tall former grain silo and which also incorporates a museum of contemporary art. They are challenged to wash the building's 96 windows inside and out, and discover how staff have coped with the challenge of keeping the place clean in the wake of the worst drought to hit Cape Town in a century

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Actor David Tennant hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with comedian Jack Dee and journalist Helen Lewis joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to look back at the week's events

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Climate Change: Ade on the Frontline
programme.desc: Ade Adepitan scours the globe for solutions to environmental problems, examining the natural and technological fixes that can help people slow the rate of decline and adapt to shifts already taking place. He begins in the Solomon Islands and then travels down the east coast of Australia from the Great Barrier Reef to Tasmania, learning about the 'feminisation' of green turtles and coming face-to-face with the terror of a bushfire

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison presents the show from the Golden Triangle in Gloucestershire, an area famous for wild daffodils. She talks to local people who remember picking the wild flowers as children and visits some of the finest medieval wall paintings in England. Charlotte Smith investigates the challenges facing rural churches and the communities they serve, and a mother and son reveal how they have fulfilled their dreams of farming in the Cumbrian hills

programme.title: Paloma Faith: As I Am
programme.desc: Documentary following the singer-songwriter as she balances the demands of a make-or-break tour with writing a new album, launching an acting career and being a first-time mum. The film observes Paloma's life both front and backstage in the midst of this pivotal year for her career, and sees her pitted against a music industry that does not always look too kindly on motherhood

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents from Christchurch Park in Ipswich, Suffolk, where treasures include an Imperial Chinese porcelain vase fit for an emperor and some intriguing portraits related to the Profumo scandal of the 1960s. Also featured are intricate, solid gold lapel badges from the Australian gold fields and a pair of delicate paintings by a famous Suffolk artist

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Patagonia: Earth's Secret Paradise
programme.desc: The documentary offering a glimpse into the rarely seen South American wilderness continues, this time following the path of the relentless wind sweeping east through Patagonia's dry desert from the Andes' peaks. The programme reveals a weird world of maras - a large relative of the guinea pig - and penguins, and witnesses the first faltering steps of baby guanacos - Patagonia's very own camels. Cameras also observe the people who have taken on this inhospitable environment and carved out a home for themselves. Santiago Cabrera narrates

programme.title: Voyages of Discovery
programme.desc: Paul Rose recounts the story of how American submarine USS Squalus became stranded in the Atlantic in 1939 after a fault caused a partial flooding of the vessel and the deaths of 26 men. The surviving 33 crew members were rescued by divers under the direction of inventor Charles Momsen, employing his pioneering underwater escape apparatus

programme.title: The Young Montalbano
programme.desc: Salvo and Livia start to prepare for their wedding and Mimi' Auguello is especially eager to be picked as Salvo's wedding witness. Meanwhile, a fire at a local hotel, which resulted in a man's death, is suspected to have been caused by arson, while the police receive an anonymous letter warning of the impending murder of a married woman at the hands of her jealous husband. In Italian

programme.title: Italy's Invisible Cities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Dr Michael Scott explore the Italian city of Venice, using 3D technology to discover how a city built in a swamp came to be one of the most powerful locations in Medieval Europe. The pair dive deep into the many canals in this unique place to experience how its buildings still remain standing to this day, and highlight how Venice's beauty once masked a ruthless secret state, and a world of excess and vice

programme.title: Masterworks
programme.desc: Michael Berkeley explores the history of Igor Stravinsky's The Rite of Spring, discovering that its 20th-century premiere ended in a riot. Conductor Leonard Slatkin leads the BBC Symphony Orchestra in a performance of the piece, complemented by choreographed routines by the Richard Alston Dance Company

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts the edition first aired on November 29, with performances by artists including Dream Warriors, Dimples D, Chris Isaak, Pet Shop Boys, Soul II Soul featuring Kym Mazelle, Megabass, Patrick Macnee and Honor Blackman, Bombalurina featuring Timmy Mallet, Vanilla Ice and Deee-Lite

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Mark Goodier hosts the show's 1,400th edition, first aired on December 6 1990. With performances by Twenty 4 Seven featuring Captain Hollywood, the Farm, Cliff Richard, Madonna, MC Hammer, Snap, New Kids on the Block, Vanilla Ice and Betty Boo

programme.title: Patagonia: Earth's Secret Paradise
programme.desc: The documentary offering a glimpse into the rarely seen South American wilderness continues, this time following the path of the relentless wind sweeping east through Patagonia's dry desert from the Andes' peaks. The programme reveals a weird world of maras - a large relative of the guinea pig - and penguins, and witnesses the first faltering steps of baby guanacos - Patagonia's very own camels. Cameras also observe the people who have taken on this inhospitable environment and carved out a home for themselves. Santiago Cabrera narrates

programme.title: Voyages of Discovery
programme.desc: Paul Rose recounts the story of how American submarine USS Squalus became stranded in the Atlantic in 1939 after a fault caused a partial flooding of the vessel and the deaths of 26 men. The surviving 33 crew members were rescued by divers under the direction of inventor Charles Momsen, employing his pioneering underwater escape apparatus

programme.title: A Year in an English Garden: Flicker & Pulse
programme.desc: Brian McClave and Tom Wichelow's striking and poignant portrayal of time passing in a beautiful walled garden in the south-east of England. Using real-time and time-lapse footage, the film explores the relationship between the seasons and the plants and people who work within the walls of the garden. Locked into the clock of the solar system, the garden performs its annual display, guided by those passionately engaged with its soil

programme.title: The British Garden: Life and Death on Your Lawn
programme.desc: Chris Packham and a group of experts spend 12 months scrutinising the wildlife in eight gardens on a suburban street in Welwyn Garden City. Throughout the seasons they find male crickets that bribe females with food during sex, spiders that change colour to help catch prey, and ferocious life-and-death battles in the compost heap. They also shows a different side to familiar garden residents, revealing how a robin's red breast is actually war paint, and a single litter of foxes can have up to five different fathers

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: A look at the hidden secrets of the universe

programme.title: Between the Lines
programme.desc: Tony investigates the conviction of an armed robber - a tricky case complicated still further by his involvement with a tenacious reporter on the trail of official corruption. Gritty drama, starring Neil Pearson

programme.title: Between the Lines
programme.desc: Tony is attacked by the brother of a picket-line protester beaten up by police officers for allegedly throwing a petrol bomb. Gritty drama, starring Neil Pearson, Tom Georgeson and Siobhan Redmond

programme.title: A Timewatch Guide
programme.desc: Historian Dan Snow explores the discovery, excavation and raising of the ship, finding out what the latest research has revealed about the vessel and her crew. The presenter also investigates how the project helped create modern underwater archaeology, and examines the techniques, challenges and successes of the divers and archaeologists involved

programme.title: A Year in an English Garden: Flicker & Pulse
programme.desc: Brian McClave and Tom Wichelow's striking and poignant portrayal of time passing in a beautiful walled garden in the south-east of England. Using real-time and time-lapse footage, the film explores the relationship between the seasons and the plants and people who work within the walls of the garden. Locked into the clock of the solar system, the garden performs its annual display, guided by those passionately engaged with its soil

programme.title: The British Garden: Life and Death on Your Lawn
programme.desc: Chris Packham and a group of experts spend 12 months scrutinising the wildlife in eight gardens on a suburban street in Welwyn Garden City. Throughout the seasons they find male crickets that bribe females with food during sex, spiders that change colour to help catch prey, and ferocious life-and-death battles in the compost heap. They also shows a different side to familiar garden residents, revealing how a robin's red breast is actually war paint, and a single litter of foxes can have up to five different fathers

programme.title: Walk the Line
programme.desc: Exploring more of Northern Ireland's most spectacular countryside, Barra Best investigates County Antrim's lost railway routes

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross creates a beautiful oval painting of an old cabin at the end of a weathered pathway

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate a small watercolour sketch that could be the work of sculptor Henry Moore. The piece was found in 2012 in a hoard of artwork stolen by the Nazis. While the unidentified piece has many characteristics of Moore's work, none of the other artworks recovered were by British artists, so it remains a mystery how it came to be there. The team must not only find out who created it, but also who it belonged to, since it may have been looted from Jewish owners during the Holocaust and will need to be returned to surviving descendants

programme.title: An Evening with Peter Alliss
programme.desc: A celebration of the golf commentator and broadcaster's life and career, featuring anecdotes from his wife Jackie, golfers Tom Watson, Darren Clarke and Gary Player, and celebrities including Gary Lineker, Terry Wogan, Chris Evans, Bruce Forsyth, Jimmy Tarbuck, Hazel Irvine and Steve Rider. Plus, clips from some of his popular programmes, including Pro-Celebrity Golf, A Round with Alliss and A Golfer's Travels. Narrated by Sue Johnston

programme.title: Around with Alliss
programme.desc: Peter Alliss enjoys a chat and a round of golf with Terry Wogan

programme.title: Art of France
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon examines the development of Impressionism, when France was changing at a rapid pace and young artists re-invented the rules of painting - finding their muses in the bars, brothels and cabarets of Paris and turning the world of art on its head. Monet, Degas and their contemporaries launched a heated debate about the role of painting in the modern world that would pave the way for just about every modern art movement of note

programme.title: The Incredible Story of Marie Antoinette's Watch with Nicholas Parsons
programme.desc: The Just a Minute host explores the life and work of Swiss horologist Abraham-Louis Breguet and goes in search of his most famous timepiece, which is valued at up to $100m

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross creates a beautiful oval painting of an old cabin at the end of a weathered pathway

programme.title: Walk the Line
programme.desc: Exploring more of Northern Ireland's most spectacular countryside, Barra Best investigates County Antrim's lost railway routes

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate a small watercolour sketch that could be the work of sculptor Henry Moore. The piece was found in 2012 in a hoard of artwork stolen by the Nazis. While the unidentified piece has many characteristics of Moore's work, none of the other artworks recovered were by British artists, so it remains a mystery how it came to be there. The team must not only find out who created it, but also who it belonged to, since it may have been looted from Jewish owners during the Holocaust and will need to be returned to surviving descendants

programme.title: Art of France
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon examines the development of Impressionism, when France was changing at a rapid pace and young artists re-invented the rules of painting - finding their muses in the bars, brothels and cabarets of Paris and turning the world of art on its head. Monet, Degas and their contemporaries launched a heated debate about the role of painting in the modern world that would pave the way for just about every modern art movement of note

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Zionne, Isaiah and Mandi are young black teenagers who have all experienced racism growing up in the UK. Mandi has been called racist names, Isaiah is often followed round shops by security guards, and Zionne feels she will always have to work harder than everyone else to succeed. In the first of a two-part special, they speak out about what life is really like growing up black in the UK and why things urgently need to change. After the death of George Floyd and the nationwide protests against racism, the teenagers know that this is their time to be heard. As well as opening up about their own experiences, they speak to their friends and their parents. They decide to create a manifesto to list the big things they feel need to change in society to make life better for black kids like them.

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Formula One superstar Lewis Hamilton joins three black teenagers on a mission to make life better for black kids growing up in the UK. Zionne, Isaiah and Mandi have all experienced racism and have come together to create a manifesto to list the big changes they want to see in society. They enlist their celebrity heroes Lewis Hamilton, Nissy Tee and DJ Sideman to help get their message out there and discover that even the most successful black people have had to overcome racism along the way. Inspired by their role models, they are determined to make a change

programme.desc: Mimi’s boyfriend, James, decides to take her on a date. She prepares for what she thinks is a romantic picnic and enlists help from CBBC’s Nikki Lilly to help bake some cupcakes for dessert. However, the date doesn’t quite go as planned as James surprises Mimi with a visit to a petting zoo. Mimi is encouraged to hold a reptile that she’s most terrified of – a snake! She manages to get James back by finding a Mexican red-knee tarantula for him to hold, but his reaction is a bit more dramatic than she anticipates. We also meet Amira and her sisters, who cook a romantic dinner for their parents - the pressure is on for them to get their nan’s favourite Jamaican dish just right!

programme.title: Planet Defenders
programme.desc: Erin Ranney is a wildlife camera operator and adventurer. She splits her time between Alaska and the Falklands and loves filming the planet’s most incredible wildlife. Erin’s mission is to film rockhopper penguins on the Falkland Islands and find out why these charismatic creatures are facing serious threats to their survival. She investigates why the rockhopper population has fallen by 80 per cent since the 1930s. Climate change is the biggest threat to the rockhoppers, causing violent storms that could see them penguins face extinction. Erin also finds out how planting tussac grass can combat climate change and discovers that what we do in our own countries can affect these penguins thousands of miles away. She talks about how we can all make a difference towards helping the planet, just by taking small steps

Mr Hammond has just got back from a canoeing trip in Alaska, and Andy is waiting for the mail to arrive. Over the radio, Jen informs Andy that the mail sack is in the Jungle Den. Mr Hammond found a bald eagle feather while in Alaska but had it sent back, so he is also waiting for the mail. Andy finds the mail sack and starts to go through it. Andy makes a terrible discovery as the seaweed he ordered for the aquatic plants collection has leaked all over the mail - including Mr Hammonds bald eagle feather. Everything is ruined! It’s time to go on an aquatic adventure! Donning his adventure gear, Andy takes to the skies in his safari-mobile and jets off to Alaska. Andy almost flies into two bald eagles fighting in mid-air, causing the safari-mobile to crash-land in

programme.title: Topsy and Tim
programme.desc: Topsy and Tim are growing out of their old, matching twin clothes so Mummy buys them new jeans, but Tim is not sure he is ready to wear bigger clothes yet

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Aleisha's family comes from Uganda. Today, she is cooking the traditional meal of rolex, which is a Ugandan omelette. Aleisha uses tasty fresh vegetables such as baby tomatoes, cabbage, red onion and her special ingredient, mild green chilli, for her omelettes. She pours whisked eggs into baking tins and puts her vegetables on top. Then her recipe is ready to be cooked for her friends to enjoy. To top off the occasion, Aleisha wears a wonderful Ugandan outfit and has a musical surprise planned for her pals. Will Aleisha's friends guess her secret ingredient?

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: It's the hottest day of the year and Waffle is eating ice cream to cool down. Outside in the sweltering heat, Simon turns on the sprinkler to help everyone cool down, but poor Waffle doesn't like the sprinkler and darts through the hole in the fence to Mrs Hobbs's garden, looking for shade. The Brooklyn-Bells try to coax him back, but he refuses to return. Doug decides to make a mini swimming pool by filling the ball pit with cool water. Waffle loves swimming pools. Maybe that will encourage him back?

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. He works there with his boss, Mr Hammond and tech wizard Jen, who monitors all the animals from the Aqua Base. Mr Hammond is giving one of his special wildlife talks at Safari World. This one is all about a seabird called a sooty tern. Jen has deployed one of her remote-controlled bird-cams, which is painted to look like a sooty tern, to a small island in the Indian Ocean. The live feed from the bird-cam will be played during Mr Hammonds talk. Jen gets the feed up on the screen to show Andy. The footage shows a young sooty tern flying across the ocean on its fledgling flight. Suddenly, the image turns to static! Mr Hammond really needs that live feed for his talk and Andy realises there’s only one thing for it. It’s time to go on an aquatic adventure! Andy runs to the Aqua Dome’s loading dock and boards his safari-sub, a hi-tech underwater submersible capable of travelli

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join Charlie, Etti and their families as they discover the world around them at three months old and learn what makes them both smile. We join Etti, who is having a fun day out with her family at some local gardens. They see lots of different flowers and animals including a big rabbit statue! But will these make Etti and her sister, Grace, smile? Meanwhile, Charlie and his family are out visiting a zoo where they see lots of different birds. Mum does an impression that makes Charlie smile, so his family use an instant camera to take some lovely family photos - but what will they do with them? After a quick stop at the local park, Etti and Grace decide to stop at a friend’s house to play peek-a-boo together. Grace creates her own happy faces with cut-out features. Finally we see what Charlie and his family were taking photos for - a photo album! What a fun and special way to look back on their family day out.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Sebastiaan's making a South African bobotie, which is a local dish his family have shared with him. He starts by putting chopped spring onions, grated courgette, turmeric powder, ground coriander and torn red pepper into a casserole dish and cooking them. He then adds garlic puree, ginger puree, chutney and chopped tomatoes to cooked beef mince. Sebastiaan then carefully makes powder out of his special ingredient, cumin seeds, and adds the powder to the beef mixture. Sebastiaan mixes both the vegetables and the beef in an oven dish and flattens them before adding two bay leaves. He adds a topping of milk and egg and then places it in the oven. Sebastiaan has a wonderfully decorated table, and he has another traditional treat to go with his bobotie. Will his friends figure out what his special ingredient is?

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. Andy has to clear all the leaves off the path in the Amazon Dome, which is a huge task, but luckily Jen has invented a new machine to help him. It’s a superpowered leaf blower! Heading to Jungle Den to find it, Andy bumps into Mr Hammond, who is unpacking from his photography expedition to the Amazon River. He managed to photograph a particularly rare fish, which has not been named yet but belongs to a group of fish called cichlids. Mr Hammond has arranged a press conference to reveal his photo but can’t work out how to get the image off the memory card. Andy offers to drop the card over to Jen after he has finished clearing the Amazon Dome. programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join nine-month-old Bonnie and Harry as they discover the world around them and become more expressive. Both children love playing with their families - laughing, smiling, clapping, playing peek-a-boo and making noise. Bonnie gets excited when her dad suggests a day out to the beach! When they get there, Bonnie is concentrating on making sandcastles before splashing her feet in the water, laughing and loving the sea. Harry and his family go on a bug hunt at a nature reserve, but what will they find? While the family enjoys a relaxing picnic, Harry tries a banana for the very first time. Bonnie is happily crawling along the beach finding seashells before mum lays out a picnic with yummy hummus and pitta bread. Before leaving, there is much laughter as they decide to try and completely cover dad with sand. After their picnic, Harry’s decides to join his brother in having his photo taken, and it’s big smiles all around!
